_meta.description: Per-pair outer switching cutoffs (r_out, Å) for the ZBL screened-Coulomb calculator.
purpose: ZBLCalculator uses these values as the pair-specific outer switching distance.
The ZBL weight S(r) transitions from 1 (fully active) at r ≤ cutoff_inner
to 0 (fully off) at r ≥ r_out stored here.
At equilibrium bond lengths (r ≈ r_out), S ≈ 0, so ZBL does not perturb
normal bonded geometries.  ZBL kicks in only at sub-bonding close contacts.

derivation.calibration: Chosen so that H–Si gives 1.5 Å, matching the Si–H single-bond length (1.480 Å).
derivation.cov_radius_source: Pyykko & Atsumi, Chem. Eur. J. 2009 (single-bond radii), consistent with src/chem_data.json

_meta.key_convention: Alphabetically sorted element symbols joined by '-', e.g. 'H-Si' not 'Si-H'.
_meta.inner_cutoff_note: The inner cutoff (where S=1, ZBL fully active) is a global parameter in ZBLCalculator, default 0.5 Å.  It is NOT stored here.
_meta.fallback: Pairs absent from this file use ZBLCalculator.cutoff_outer (global fallback, default 2.5 Å).
